research_topic: Agent Skills Best Practices, Structure, and Design Patterns

definition_and_purpose.what_is_a_skill: An agent skill is a reusable, packaged unit of domain-specific knowledge, capability, or procedural expertise designed to enhance an AI agent's effectiveness. Unlike generic documentation, a skill is agent-first: it's structured, discoverable, and intended for agents to locate, invoke, and leverage during execution. A skill encapsulates specialized knowledge (how to perform a technical task), procedural patterns (step-by-step workflows), or reference material (APIs, tools, best practices) in a machine-readable and human-readable format.
purpose: Enable agents to discover and reuse specialized knowledge without duplicating information
Provide agents with procedural guidance for complex, domain-specific tasks
Serve as a reference layer that agents can query (recall) during problem-solving
Package knowledge artifacts that agents need but wouldn't know to look for
Create composable capability blocks that multiple agents can leverage

skills_vs_documentation.skill: Agent-first, discoverable, structured: includes metadata (keywords, audience, prerequisites), clear entry points, explicit when-to-use guidance. Designed for agents to find and reference during execution.
skills_vs_documentation.guide: Human-first, descriptive, narrative: comprehensive walkthrough of a topic. Read end-to-end. Not optimized for programmatic discovery.
skills_vs_documentation.reference: Human-first, organized by structure: API docs, parameter tables, type definitions. Humans search for specific information. Not a guided walkthrough.
skills_vs_documentation.research: Synthetic findings with sources: deep analysis of patterns, best practices, and recommendations. Historical context and credibility assessment.

example_2_hindsight_docs.description: Complete Hindsight documentation packaged as an agent skill
example_2_hindsight_docs.structure: Frontmatter (name, description) + organized documentation directory with references, SDKs, integrations, cookbook. Designed for agents to navigate and extract relevant sections.
example_2_hindsight_docs.key_insight: Shows that a skill can wrap extensive reference documentation and provide clear navigation paths for agents to find what they need.

example_2_cli_modes_redesign.description: CLI Modes Skill (being redesigned with proper structure)
example_2_cli_modes_redesign.status: UNDER REDESIGN (deleted 2026-03-31 confused version, rebuilding with proper design)
example_2_cli_modes_redesign.plan: Rebuild with: clear frontmatter, focused entry point, navigation to comprehensive guides
example_2_cli_modes_redesign.key_insight: Initial version was too large and guide-like, conflating skill and guide. New version will be entry point + navigation.

example_3_claude_features.description: Anthropic's Claude documentation as model capabilities skill
example_3_claude_features.pattern: When Claude agents need to know what Claude can do (tools, limits, features), this is packaged as a discoverable skill with model capabilities, limitations, and versioning.
example_3_claude_features.key_insight: Skills are effective for packaging capabilities documentation that agents need during reasoning.

name.description: Human-readable skill name. Should be searchable and descriptive.
name.best_practice: Use clear, domain-specific names. Avoid generic names like 'Utilities' or 'Helpers'.

description.description: 1-2 sentence summary of what the skill provides and when agents should use it.
examples: Complete Hindsight documentation for AI agents. Use this to learn about architecture, APIs, and best practices.
Reference guide for GitHub Copilot CLI modes and when to use each mode.
description.best_practice: Be specific about the skill's domain and use case. Agents will use this to decide if the skill is relevant to their current task.

keywords.description: Search terms agents might use when looking for this skill
examples: hindsight, memory, retention, recall, reflect, documentation, agents
keywords.best_practice: Include synonyms, alternative names, and related concepts. Think about how agents would ask for this knowledge.

audience.description: Which agents/roles should use this skill
audience.best_practice: Be explicit about who benefits from this skill. Helps orchestrators route work appropriately.

prerequisites.description: Required knowledge or skills agents need before using this skill
prerequisites.best_practice: Clear prerequisites prevent agents from misusing the skill or getting lost in advanced material.

difficulty.best_practice: Helps agents self-select appropriate resources. Mix levels in skill structure so agents can start simple.

version.description: Semantic version (1.0.0) for tracking skill evolution
version.best_practice: Enables version-aware references and deprecation tracking.

tags.description: Categorical tags for navigation and filtering
tags.best_practice: Use consistent tagging scheme. Enables cross-skill discovery.

markdown_content_structure.description: Clear statement of WHEN and WHY agents should use this skill
markdown_content_structure.content:
- What problem does this skill solve?
- When should agents reference this skill?
- What will agents learn/accomplish?

markdown_content_structure.description: How agents can navigate the skill. Multiple pathways for different use cases
markdown_content_structure.content:
- Start here: [link to best practices]
- Find X by [navigation method]
- Quick reference: [summary table]
- Full API: [comprehensive reference]

markdown_content_structure.description: Key ideas agents need to understand (brief, not comprehensive)
markdown_content_structure.content:
- Concept 1: Definition + quick context
- Concept 2: Definition + quick context
- Links to detailed sections for deep dives

markdown_content_structure.description: Procedural guidance: step-by-step for common tasks
markdown_content_structure.content:
- Task 1: Step-by-step workflow
- Task 2: Step-by-step workflow
- Common variations

- API endpoints / parameters
- Configuration options
- Code examples
- Best practices

markdown_content_structure.description: How to combine this skill with others for common workflows
markdown_content_structure.content:
- Pattern 1: How to use this skill with [other skill]
- Pattern 2: Multi-step workflow combining skills

minimal_viable_skill.definition: Minimum a skill needs to provide actionable value to agents
required_components: One primary use case + entry point
Links to deeper material if complexity requires it
minimal_viable_skill.structure: Frontmatter + 2-3 tight sections. Assume agent knows context; don't over-explain.
minimal_viable_skill.when_appropriate: Narrow domain with clear singular use case, common reference material, temporary/evolving knowledge

signs: Single .md file >5000 words (should split or link to guides)
Trying to cover everything (becomes unwieldy for agents searching)
Heavy narrative prose (agents scan, don't read essays)
Unclear when to use this skill (too broad)
No clear entry points or navigation (agents get lost)
Assumes agents read top-to-bottom (modern agents navigate laterally)
overweight_skill_antipattern.solution: Split into focused skills. Core skill (1000-2000 words) + linked guides (reading) + linked references (looking up details)

comprehensive_skill.definition: Well-structured skill with both breadth and depth
structure: Frontmatter with rich metadata (keywords, audience, prerequisites, difficulty)
Overview + entry point guidance (helps agents navigate)
Multiple entry points for different use cases
Linked subdirectories with detailed reference material
comprehensive_skill.when_appropriate: Complex domain, multiple use cases, deep reference material needed, foundational to multiple agents
comprehensive_skill.example: hindsight-docs skill with references/, SDKs/, integrations/, cookbook/ subdirectories

size_guidelines.under_500_words: Too brief. Not enough value. Grow it or leave it as summary in a guide.
size_guidelines.500_1500_words: Minimal viable skill. Good for narrow, well-defined domains. Entry point + core concept + one example.
size_guidelines.1500_3000_words: Sweet spot for most skills. Covers domain with breadth and depth. Multiple entry points, examples, patterns.
size_guidelines.3000_5000_words: Complex domain with multiple use cases. Consider whether this should be multiple skills.
size_guidelines.over_5000_words: Either split into multiple skills or link to guides. Skill is too heavy for agent navigation.

split_decision_framework.question_1: Are there distinct use cases with different prerequisites?
split_decision_framework.if_yes: Split into focused skills. Example: 'Hindsight for Beginners' vs 'Hindsight API Reference'
split_decision_framework.question_2: Would agents ask different questions about this topic?
split_decision_framework.if_yes: Split by question. Example: 'CLI Modes Overview' vs 'CLI Mode Comparison Table'
split_decision_framework.question_3: Is there supporting material (deep dives, tutorial, etc)?
split_decision_framework.if_yes: Link to guides/references. Keep skill as entry point and navigation layer.
split_decision_framework.heuristic: If you imagine an agent asking 'How do I do X with this skill?' and can't answer clearly in <500 words, split it.

how_agents_find_skills: Explicit invocation: @skill-name (if agent knows skill exists)
Orchestrator routing: Ben analyzes task and provides hint: 'Use hindsight-docs to understand memory patterns'
Semantic search: Agent uses semantic_search or grep to find relevant skills by topic
Directory enumeration: Agent lists .github/agents/skills/ and reads frontmatter to discover available skills
Metadata matching: Agent searches by keyword from skill metadata
how_agents_invoke_skills: Read entire skill + navigate by section: Agent needs comprehensive understanding
Extract specific section: Agent asks 'What does this section say about X?' and references just that part
Use entry point: Agent follows 'Start Here' guidance to navigate to relevant subsection
Compose with other skills: Agent uses skill A + skill B together to solve complex problem
Reference during reasoning: Agent includes skill excerpt in reasoning context to improve decision-making

cross_linking_patterns.skill_to_skill: Skills link to each other when they're composable: 'See [Agent Architecture Skill] for context on how agents work'
cross_linking_patterns.skill_to_guide: Skill links to guides for deeper learning: 'For detailed tutorial, see docs/guides/hindsight-comprehensive-guide.md'
cross_linking_patterns.skill_to_research: Skill links to research for best practices/patterns: 'See docs/research/agentic-workflows/ for pattern analysis'
cross_linking_patterns.metadata_enables_discovery: Keywords and tags in frontmatter enable agents to find related skills without explicit links

metadata_for_programmatic_discovery.keywords_field: Enables semantic matching: agent searching for 'memory operations' finds hindsight skill
metadata_for_programmatic_discovery.audience_field: Helps orchestrators route: '@research agent needs historical context? Check audience field.'
metadata_for_programmatic_discovery.prerequisites_field: Prevents misuse: agent checks if it has context needed before diving into advanced skill
metadata_for_programmatic_discovery.tags_field: Enables category filtering: 'Show me all pattern: tags' or 'category: memory'
metadata_for_programmatic_discovery.difficulty_field: Helps agents select learning path: start with beginner section before advanced

skill.purpose: Agent-optimized packaged knowledge with clear entry points and navigation
skill.audience: AI agents and users who know they want this specific domain
skill.structure: YAML frontmatter + organized sections with nav guidance + linked references
skill.length: 500-5000 words (entry point + core content, not exhaustive)
skill.discovery: Keywords, tags, metadata. Agents search for skills explicitly.
skill.reading_pattern: Non-linear: agents jump to relevant sections based on task

guide.purpose: Comprehensive walkthrough/tutorial of a topic. Human-readable learning path
guide.audience: Humans learning a new domain. Also agents doing research/context-building
guide.structure: Chapter/section structure that flows logically from intro → advanced
guide.discovery: Linked from skills, referenced in docs/guides/ directory
guide.reading_pattern: Linear/sequential: readers follow learning path chapter by chapter

reference.purpose: Lookup material: API docs, parameters, type definitions, lookup tables
reference.audience: Developers/agents looking up specific details while building something
reference.structure: Organized alphabetically or by category. Each entry is self-contained.
reference.length: Variable: entries are concise (parameter definition = 1-2 sentences)
reference.discovery: Indexed, searchable, often auto-generated from code

research.purpose: Synthesized findings with sources. Patterns, best practices, recommendations
research.discovery: Referenced in architecture docs, linked from relevant skills/guides
research.reading_pattern: Summary-first: read exec summary + recommendations, deep-dive into findings if needed

mature_system_patterns: Anthropic Claude docs: API reference (reference) + guides (tutorial walkthrough) + cookbook (patterns)
VS Code docs: Getting started (guides) + API reference (reference) + Extension examples (patterns)
Kubernetes docs: Concepts (guides) + API reference (reference) + Tasks (how-to guides)
Hindsight docs skill: Architecture (guide) + API reference (reference) + SDK integrations (patterns) + Cookbook (recipes)

separation_of_concerns.skill: Navigation layer + entry points. 'Here's how to learn this domain'
separation_of_concerns.guide: Learning path. 'Start here and follow this path to understand'
separation_of_concerns.reference: Lookup layer. 'Here's the specific detail you're looking for'
separation_of_concerns.research: Analysis layer. 'Here's what we learned from investigating this'

directory_structure_recommendation.proposed_structure: `.github/agents/skills/` contains skill packages, each with: SKILL.md (entry point) + README.md (extended guide if complex) + subdirectories as needed
directory_structure_recommendation.rationale: Skills are the discovery layer. Guides/references live in docs/. This keeps skills focused on entry points and navigation.
directory_structure_recommendation.example: `.github/agents/skills/hindsight-docs/` with: SKILL.md (agent entry point) + structured subdirectories (references/, sdks/, cookbook/)

anatomy_of_reusable_skill: Clear domain boundary: 'This skill is about X, not Y'
Atomic knowledge unit: Skill stands alone; doesn't require hours of prerequisite reading
Discoverable: Keywords, tags, and metadata enable finding it. Frontmatter tells agents when to use it
Entry points: Multiple ways in depending on agent's starting question
Composable: Can be combined with other skills to solve larger problems
Versioned: Semantic versioning enables tracking changes and deprecations

skill_composition_patterns.pattern_1_sequential: Agent uses skill A to learn X, then skill B to apply X. Example: Learn agent architecture (skill) → use that to understand custom agents (skill)
skill_composition_patterns.pattern_2_parallel: Agent uses skill A + skill B simultaneously for different aspects of problem. Example: CLI modes (skill) + agent architecture (skill) for designing agent that uses CLI
skill_composition_patterns.pattern_3_layered: Skill A is entry point, skills B and C decompose specifics. Example: Agent architecture overview (A) → leads to vs-code-specific-patterns (B) + multi-agent-orchestration (C)
skill_composition_patterns.pattern_4_reference: Agent reads main skill, then checks reference skill for specific details. Example: Hindsight architecture (skill) + API reference (linked from skill)

reusability_anti_patterns: Coupling to specific agent: 'This skill is only for @doc agent' → Not reusable. Create domain skill, not agent-specific skill
Assumed context: 'You already know how agents work' → Not reusable for new agents. State prerequisites in metadata
Hidden structure: No table of contents, no clear sections → Agents can't navigate efficiently
Mixed concerns: Skill about CLI modes + skill about agent design → Split into focused skills
No keywords/tags: How would an agent find this? → Agents can't discover it

best_practices_dos.practice: Start with clear metadata (name, description, keywords)
best_practices_dos.why: Agents need to know what your skill is about before reading. Metadata determines discoverability.
best_practices_dos.example: name: 'CLI Modes Reference', keywords: ['cli modes', 'copilot cli', 'github copilot cli modes']

best_practices_dos.practice: Include clear entry points and navigation guidance
best_practices_dos.why: Agents don't read documents end-to-end. They jump to relevant sections. Help them navigate.
best_practices_dos.example: 'Looking to pick a CLI mode? See Mode Selection Table. Want API parameters? See Reference. Just learning? Start with Concepts.'

best_practices_dos.practice: Write for agent discovery: use keywords agents would search for
best_practices_dos.why: Agents will use semantic search or grep. Think like an agent asking a question.
best_practices_dos.example: If agents ask 'How do I run Copilot in different modes?' → include 'modes', 'run', 'configurations' in keywords

best_practices_dos.why: Agents learn by example. Procedural content needs concrete instances showing what 'correct' looks like.
best_practices_dos.example: Don't just explain 'configure memory banks'. Show: 'bank = create_bank(id='my-agent', mission='...'). Then use bank in retain() calls.'

best_practices_dos.practice: Link to guides and references, don't duplicate content
best_practices_dos.why: Skill is entry point and navigation, not complete encyclopaedia. Link to comprehensive guides.

best_practices_dos.practice: State prerequisites and target audience explicitly
best_practices_dos.why: Prevents agents from getting lost in material they're not ready for. Helps orchestrators route appropriately.
best_practices_dos.example: Prerequisites: Understanding of agent architecture. Target audience: Agents designing orchestration workflows.

best_practices_dos.practice: Use consistent section headers and structure across skills
best_practices_dos.why: Agents recognize patterns. Consistent structure makes skills predictable and easier to navigate.
best_practices_dos.example: All skills follow: Overview + When to Use + How to Use + Common Patterns + Reference

best_practices_dos.why: Skills evolve. Agents and users need to know if this skill is current or deprecated.
best_practices_dos.example: version: 1.2.0, status: active, deprecated_in: null. Or: status: deprecated_in 2.0, see new-skill for replacement

anti_patterns_donts.antipattern: Writing skill as if it's a guide (narrative, complete encyclopaedia)
anti_patterns_donts.problem: Makes skill too heavy for agent navigation. Agents get lost. Skill becomes 'read all or nothing'
anti_patterns_donts.instead: Write skill as entry point layer. Narrative content goes in docs/guides/

anti_patterns_donts.antipattern: Unclear when to use skill ('this is about stuff')
anti_patterns_donts.problem: Agents can't determine if this is relevant to their task. Wasted discovery cost.
anti_patterns_donts.instead: State clearly: 'Use this when you need to [specific problem]. Don't use this for [what it's NOT about]'

anti_patterns_donts.antipattern: Mixing multiple concerns in one skill (agent design + tool patterns + orchestration)
anti_patterns_donts.problem: Makes skill unmaintainable and confusing. What's the domain boundary?
anti_patterns_donts.instead: One skill per focused domain. Cross-link related skills.

anti_patterns_donts.antipattern: No keywords or metadata ('if agents want it, they'll find it somehow')
anti_patterns_donts.problem: Skills are undiscoverable. Agents can't find them. Work gets duplicated.
anti_patterns_donts.instead: Rich metadata is mandatory. Keywords, audience, prerequisites, tags enable discovery.

anti_patterns_donts.antipattern: Assuming agent context ('You already know how X works')
anti_patterns_donts.problem: Skill isn't self-contained. Agents can't use it without external training.
anti_patterns_donts.instead: State prerequisites explicitly. Assume minimal context.

anti_patterns_donts.antipattern: No clear structure or table of contents
anti_patterns_donts.problem: Agents can't navigate. Have to read everything to find what they need.
anti_patterns_donts.instead: Structure with sections, internal links, and nav guidance. Make scanning easy.

anti_patterns_donts.problem: Not reusable. Other agents can't leverage. Defeats the purpose of packaging.
anti_patterns_donts.instead: Create domain skills (@doc uses them, but so can other agents)

anti_patterns_donts.antipattern: Outdated or abandoned skills with no version/deprecation info
anti_patterns_donts.problem: Agents use stale information. Creates confusion and errors.
anti_patterns_donts.instead: Version all skills. Mark deprecated skills clearly. Link to replacements.

directory_structure.rationale: Skills are organized by domain (cli-modes, hindsight-docs, agent-architecture). Each skill has a SKILL.md entry point + optional supporting files.
directory_structure.example_tree:
`.github/agents/skills/
├── hindsight-docs/
│   ├── SKILL.md
│   ├── references/
│   ├── sdks/
│   └── cookbook/
├── agent-architecture/
│   ├── SKILL.md
│   └── ...
└── README.md (index of all skills)`

naming_conventions.rationale: Matches common package naming conventions. Easy to reference in documentation.
naming_conventions.skill_file: Always SKILL.md (uppercase) inside skill directory. Makes pattern clear.

navigation_discoverability.skills_index: Create `.github/agents/skills/README.md` that lists all skills with: name, description, keywords, audience, link
navigation_discoverability.rationale: Simple navigation for agents discovering available skills. One place to see all options.
navigation_discoverability.metadata_in_index: Extract metadata from skill frontmatter automatically (if possible) or maintain manually
navigation_discoverability.example_index:
| Skill | Description | Keywords | For |
|-------|---|---|---|
| CLI Modes | Reference guide for Copilot CLI modes | cli, modes, github copilot | Any agent using Copilot CLI |

documentation_hierarchy_mapping.reference_layer: `docs/research/` + linked reference files → Detailed lookup material and patterns
documentation_hierarchy_mapping.research_layer: `.github/context/` → Research findings with sources (not consumed by agents directly, but informs skill design)

cross_linking.skill_to_guides: SKILL.md says: 'For comprehensive tutorial, see docs/guides/[guide-name].md'
cross_linking.guide_to_skills: Guides say: 'Reference this with @skill-name during agent workflows'
cross_linking.skills_to_each_other: Related skills link to each other in 'See Also' section
cross_linking.all_layers: Each layer links down to supporting material, up to discovery layer

maintenance_responsibility.skill_owner: @skill-builder agent maintains skill quality, structure, metadata
maintenance_responsibility.reference_owner: Whoever maintains that subsystem keeps reference current (e.g., CLI maintainer updates modes reference)

version_control_patterns.skills_in_git: SKILL.md and supporting files are version-controlled
version_control_patterns.semantic_versioning: Each skill has version field. Bump when updating.
version_control_patterns.changelog: Maintain simple CHANGELOG or version annotation in SKILL.md
version_control_patterns.deprecation_strategy: Don't delete dead skills. Mark deprecated. Link to replacement.

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.recommendation: Adopt consistent SKILL.md structure across all skills
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Consistency makes skills predictable. Agents and users recognize patterns.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Define standard SKILL.md template with required sections: Frontmatter, Overview, When to Use, How to Use, Patterns, Reference

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.recommendation: Require rich frontmatter metadata (keywords, audience, prerequisites, difficulty, tags)
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Metadata enables programmatic discovery and routing. Without it, skills are hidden.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Create template with frontmatter requirements. @skill-builder enforces in reviews.

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: One place for agents and users to discover available skills. Improves discoverability.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Generate index from skill metadata. Update it whenever new skill created.

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Clear separation of concerns prevents duplication and confusion.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Document hierarchy in workspace docs. Use in skill design reviews.

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.recommendation: Target 1500-2500 words for most new skills
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Sweet spot: comprehensive enough to be useful, lightweight enough for agent navigation.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Use word count as quality gate. If >3000 words, consider splitting.

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.recommendation: Always include 'When to Use' and 'Common Patterns' sections
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Helps agents determine relevance and understand composability.

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.recommendation: Link heavy reference material as supporting files, not inline
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Keeps skill entry point lean. Agents can drill down for details as needed.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Move >1000-word reference sections to separate [topic]-reference.md or subdirectory

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Keywords determine discoverability. Need clear process for identifying them.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Document: 'Think about how agents would ask for this. Put those terms in keywords.'

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Ensures new skills follow standards and best practices.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Create .github/agents/skills/SKILL-TEMPLATE.md with required sections, fields, word count target

design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.recommendation: Implement semantic caching for skill metadata
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.rationale: Multiple agents might search for same skills. Cache metadata for performance.
design_recommendations_for_this_workspac.action: Consider storing skill metadata in .github/context/ for easy programmatic access

sources.title: Workspace: Hindsight Documentation Skill
sources.credibility: High - Production example in workspace of well-structured comprehensive skill

sources.title: Workspace: Skill-Builder Agent Instructions
sources.credibility: High - Defines skill design expectations for this workspace

sources.title: Workspace: VS Code Agent File Specification
sources.credibility: High - Documents agent format and metadata patterns

sources.title: Anthropic Claude API Documentation
sources.credibility: Very High - Official documentation from Claude creators

sources.title: VS Code Copilot Documentation
sources.credibility: Very High - Official VS Code documentation

summary: Agent skills are reusable, packaged units of domain-specific knowledge designed for agent discovery and leverage. Unlike guides (human-readable walkthroughs) or references (parameter lookup), skills are agent-first with discoverable metadata, clear entry points, and navigable structure. Effective skills have rich frontmatter (name, description, keywords, audience, prerequisites), multiple entry points for different use cases, and clear boundaries to domain expertise. Target 1500-2500 words for most skills—substantial enough to be useful, lightweight enough for agent navigation. Skills should always include 'When to Use' and 'Common Patterns' sections to help agents determine relevance and composability. Documentation hierarchy distinguishes: skills (entry points), guides (learning paths), references (lookup), and research (synthesized findings). This workspace should establish consistent SKILL.md structure, require rich metadata, create a skills index, and distinguish skill/guide/reference boundaries to prevent duplication and enable discoverability.
follow_up_questions: How should metadata be extracted/validated? Create schema validator or manual checks?
Should skill metadata be cached in .github/context/ for programmatic agent access?
How frequently should skills be reviewed/refreshed? Annual? Event-driven?
What's the skill discovery API? How do agents programmatically find relevant skills?
Should deprecated skills be archived in separate directory or marked inline?
